title: Lebanon 2026
subtitle: Wonders, Vibes and an Exponential Summer

slides.title: Why Lebanon, Why Now
bullets: A country the size of a coastline, packed with 5000 years of history
Ski the mountains in the morning, swim the Mediterranean by afternoon
Tourism rebounding fast as the diaspora and the world return
Energy that is young, creative and unstoppable

slides.title: The Ancient Wonders
slides.content: Living history you can walk through.
bullets: Baalbek: the colossal Roman temples of Jupiter and Bacchus
Byblos: one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities on Earth
Jeita Grotto: surreal underground rivers and crystal caverns
Qadisha Valley and the ancient Cedars of God
Tyre and Sidon: Phoenician roots by the sea

slides.title: Beirut Reborn
bullets: Mar Mikhael and Gemmayze: rooftop bars and late nights
World-class food scene from street manousheh to fine dining
Art galleries, design studios and a booming music culture
Resilient, glamorous and full of life

slides.title: Summer 2026 Vibes
slides.content: The Mediterranean summer at its best.
bullets: Beach clubs in Batroun, Jbeil and the Jiyeh coast
Sunset parties and open-air dance floors
Mountain escapes in Faraya, Ehden and the Shouf
Long lunches, arak, mezze and sea breeze

slides.title: Festivals of 2026
slides.content: Music and culture under the stars.
bullets: Baalbeck International Festival in the Roman ruins
Beiteddine Art Festival in a 19th century palace
Byblos International Festival by the old harbor
Wine, food and village summer fairs across the country

slides.title: Food and Wine
slides.content: One of the great cuisines of the world.
bullets: Tabbouleh, hummus, kibbeh, fresh fish and endless mezze
Bekaa Valley wineries: Ksara, Kefraya, Massaya and more
Street food culture: manousheh, knefeh and shawarma
Farm to table villages in the mountains

slides.title: Adventure and Nature
bullets: The Lebanon Mountain Trail from north to south
Hiking the Qadisha and rafting in spring rivers
Skiing the cedars in winter, diving the coast in summer
Nature reserves in the Shouf and Tannourine

slides.title: Exponential Lebanon
bullets: A young, multilingual and entrepreneurial population
Growing tech and startup scene powered by global diaspora
Surging tourism numbers driving recovery
Creativity and resilience turning into real growth

slides.title: Plan Your Summer 2026
slides.content: The time to come is now.
bullets: Best season: June to September for sun, sea and festivals
Base in Beirut, explore by day trips across the whole country
Mix history, beach, mountain and nightlife in one week
Lebanon is waiting. See you this summer.
